meta_title: For Students: design with AI to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Design with AI to Solve Debugging Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Master AI-driven debugging. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to conquer complex code errors and elevate your engineering portfolio projects.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at red error logs. Transform your debugging process by using AI to map architectural flaws rather than just patching symptoms. By integrating Antigravity for visual system design and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you shift from reactive troubleshooting to proactive software engineering, ensuring your student projects are robust and production-ready.
content.intro:
You’re staring at your screen at 2 AM. The compiler is screaming, your logic feels like a house of cards, and you're trapped in a cycle of 'print-statement-debugging' that leads nowhere. It’s the universal wall every student hits—the moment where passion meets the cold, hard reality of complex system failures. You aren't just stuck; you're experiencing the friction of transitioning from a coder to an engineer.

But what if you could change the game? Instead of fighting the syntax, you begin designing the solution. By leveraging AI as a high-level architect rather than a glorified autocomplete, you dismantle the 'can't debug' barrier. This workflow turns your most frustrating bugs into a masterclass in architecture, setting you apart from the crowd with code that is clean, documented, and resilient.

steps.title: Visualize the System Architecture
steps.description: Before touching code, map the logic flow. Use Antigravity to visualize how your data moves through the application to identify where the state corruption occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and generate a sequence diagram highlighting the data flow for the authentication module. Identify potential race conditions.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Points
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a targeted scan of your codebase, focusing on the specific error stack trace to locate the root cause of the crash.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the error log provided, create a dependency graph showing which modules interact with the failing function.

steps.title: Architectural Refactoring
steps.description: Don't just fix the line; fix the pattern. Use AI to suggest a more robust design pattern, such as moving from imperative logic to a functional or state-machine approach.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactor for the current state management logic that eliminates the identified race conditions.

steps.title: Automated Unit Testing
steps.description: Ensure the fix holds by generating comprehensive tests. Use Claude Code to create edge-case scenarios that target your specific bug.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of Jest unit tests that specifically target the edge cases identified in the previous architectural review.

steps.title: Final Documentation and Clean-up
steps.description: Document your fix so you understand the 'why' behind the 'what.' This creates a record of your engineering growth for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write technical documentation for this fix, explaining the architectural shift and the reasoning for the new pattern.

pro_tips: Never ask AI to 'fix this code.' Instead, ask it to 'explain the state transition that led to this exception' to build your own mental model.
Use Antigravity to generate sequence diagrams for every major feature; if you can't diagram it, you can't debug it.
Treat your error logs as data sets. Feed them into Claude Code as context files to uncover recurring patterns in your own coding style.
When debugging, force the AI to suggest two different architectural approaches before choosing the implementation.
Always commit your 'broken' state to a separate branch before letting the AI refactor, so you can perform a diff-review of the logic changes.
content.next_steps: Now that you have solved the immediate bug, integrate this AI-first debugging loop into your CI/CD pipeline and start building your next complex feature module.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student struggling with errors; you are an architect building scalable, AI-assisted software. Take control of your development lifecycle and turn every bug into a breakthrough. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
